rationale_for_baseline_tier: Primary tension-type headache with a negative secondary-headache red-flag screen is benign and self-managed with simple analgesia and trigger management, with routine review if frequent (T1). Thunderclap onset/meningism/focal deficits carry a hard 000/ED (T5) safety-net; GCA/positional features route to urgent review.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden 'thunderclap' worst-ever headache, weakness or numbness, slurred speech, vision loss, fever with a stiff neck or rash, seizure, or a headache clearly worse on waking/lying down
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggest subarachnoid haemorrhage, meningitis, stroke or raised ICP — emergencies.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a sudden thunderclap worst-ever headache, weakness/numbness, slurred speech, vision loss, a fever with a stiff neck or rash, or a seizure — call 000 or go to ED immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear headache red flags for self-monitoring; address stress supports.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a tension headache — benign and usually gone within a day or two. Use simple pain relief sparingly, do neck/shoulder stretches and screen breaks, and manage stress. See your GP if these become frequent.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with GP review if frequent; 000/ED for red-flag features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000/ED for thunderclap headache, focal deficits or meningism at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Tension-type headache; secondary-headache red flags screened negative; simple analgesia (used sparingly) + trigger management; safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring a new severe headache without a secondary-headache red-flag screen/safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A subarachnoid haemorrhage or meningitis is mislabelled as a tension headache and the patient deteriorates.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/imaging for a classic tension headache with a fully negative red-flag screen — unnecessary.
